publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-10-05 00:14:35 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Dr. Konstantin Frank (July 4, 1899-September 6, 1985) was a viticulturist and pioneering winemaker of the 1950s in the Finger Lakes region of New York originally from Odessa, Ukraine. The Dr. Konstantin Frank Winery records (1938-2012, bulk 1968-2007) predominantly contains newspapers and magazines regarding the Dr. Konstantin Frank Vinifera Wine Cellars in Hammondsport, New York. The collection also contains correspondence, clippings, awards and certificates, film slides and negatives, vineyard promotional and marketing materials, and ephemera.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Dr. Konstantin Frank (July 4, 1899-September 6, 1985) was a viticulturist and pioneering winemaker of the 1950s in the Finger Lakes region of New York. Born in Odessa, Ukraine, then part of the Russian Empire, Frank received his Ph.D. in Viticulture from the Odessa Polytechnic Institute in 1930. His thesis focused on techniques for growing Vitis vinifera in a cold climate. After earning his Ph.D., he was assigned to a 35,000 acre state-owned vineyard, the Troubetzkoy Experimental Grape and Wine Station, in Soviet Georgia. The Frank family left the Soviet Union in 1944, living for a time in Austria before immigrating to the United States in 1951. He then moved with his family to the Cornell University Geneva Experimental Station in 1953.

In 1954, Frank was hired by Gold Seal Winery to serve as a consultant and director of vineyard research. He established a Vitis vinifera grape nursery in order to prove the legitimacy of his claims that he could grow Vitis vinifera, the traditional grapes of European winemakers, in the United States. In 1957, Frank became a U.S. citizen, and in 1958, he purchased 188 acres of land on the western shore of Keuka Lake outside of Hammondsport, New York. In 1961, Frank retired from Gold Seal in order to dedicate his time to his own winemaking goals. In 1962, Frank founded Vinifera Wine Cellars. The first vintage was released that same year with a Riesling made from botrytized grapes. The winery quickly earned a reputation for its Rieslings and its original plantings of vines formed the backbone of New York's world-class wines and champagnes.

In 1985, Frank died at the age of 86. His legacy is cherished in New York, paving the way for other Finger Lakes producers to grow European grape varieties. In the 23 years that Frank owned his winery, he planted over 60 different varieties of European grape to prove to the world that the eastern United States could grow vinifera grape varieties. In November 2001, he was inducted into the Wine Spectator Hall of Fame for his outstanding achievement in the field of viticulture.

Konstantin's son, Willibald "Willy" Frank, became president after his father's death. He purchased a historic field stone house and restored it into a sparkling winery and named it Chateau Frank. In 1993, Fred Frank, Konstantin's grandson, succeeded his father as president of Dr. Konstantin Frank Vinifera Wine Cellars. The same year, he introduced a value line called "Salmon Run" using grapes sourced from local farms around the Finger Lakes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: The Dr. Konstantin Frank Winery records (1938-2012, bulk 1968-2007) predominantly contains newspapers and magazines regarding the Dr. Konstantin Frank Vinifera Wine Cellars in Hammondsport, New York. The collection also contains correspondence, clippings, awards and certificates, film slides and negatives of vineyard operations in the 1960s and 1970s, vineyard promotional and marketing materials, and ephemera pertaining to Dr. Frank's wines and winemaking in the Finger Lakes region of New York State.
Dr. Frank's personal copy of Phillip Wagner's American Wines & Wine-Making book, with some underlining, cataloged separately.

odd.head: General Note
odd.p: Forms part of the Eastern Wine and Grape Archive. (EWGA)

odd.head: General Note
odd.p: See call # Rare TP557.W13 1956 for Franks copy of American Wines and Wine-Making.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Dr. Konstantin Frank Winery records, #3256. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library

custodhist.head: Ownership and Custodial History
custodhist.p: Portions from the Nelson Shaulis collection.

controlaccess.subject: Grape industry.
Viticulture.
Wine and wine making -- New York.
Viticulturists.
Wineries -- New York (State) -- Hammondsport.
controlaccess.corpname: Dr. Konstantin Frank Vinifera Wine Cellars (Hammondsport, N.Y.)

did.unittitle: Series I. Frank Family and Vineyard Materials

container.altrender: Oversize Legal Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 15.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924113901924]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, reciepts, and handwritten notes. Includes a letter from the National Catholic Welfare Conference about work placement in the United States and emigration services, 1951.

container.altrender: Oversize Legal Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 15.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924113901924]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 2003 New York Wine Industry Workshop Proceedings booklet, excerpts from books on wineries and winemaking in the United States.

container.altrender: Oversize Legal Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 15.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924113901924]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newspaper and magazine clippings relating to Dr. Frank and Willy Frank, including obituaries. Also includes pahmphets and bulletins about plant breeding, agriculture, and viticulture in French, German, and Russian.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Oversize and laminated magazine and newspaper clippings.

container.altrender: Oversize Legal Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 15.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924113901924]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes pamphlets, hunting and fishing licences, and advertising materials. Many of the materials were pulled out of books from the Dr. Frank book collection at Geneva.

container.altrender: Oversize Legal Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 15.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924113901924]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An exhibitor's manual and other loose materials from the 2002 New York City Fancy Food show.

container.altrender: Oversize Legal Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 15.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924113901924]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Slides and negatives of vineyard operations and grape varieties.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: German to English an German to French dictionaries.

did.unittitle: Dr. Konstantin Frank Vinifera Wine Cellars promotional and marketing materials

container.altrender: Oversize Legal Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 15.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924113901924]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two press kit folders from the Dr. Konstantin Frank Vinifera Wine Cellars. Folders include copies of newspaper and magazine articles, certificates, photos, and pamphlets. Includes other loose promotional and marketing materials. Folder 9 includes "Winemaster," a biography of Dr. Frank from 1983.

did.unittitle: Dr. Konstantin Frank Vinifera Wine Cellars large promotional and marketing materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Posters, laminated pamphlets and articles, and certificates from the Finger Lakes Wine Growers Association to Dr. Frank.

container.altrender: Oversize Legal Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 15.25)
container.label: Computer Disks [31924113901924]

did.unittitle: Dr. Konstantin Frank Vinifera Wine Cellars Information DVD

container.altrender: Oversize Legal Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 15.25)
container.label: Computer Disks [31924113901924]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documentation on Dr. Frank's Winery, Le Chalet, "Live" with Regis and Kelly, CNBC, Seller to Cellar, Simply Wine with Andrea Immer.

did.unittitle: Dr. Konstantin Frank Vinifera Wine Cellars Information DVD [copy]

container.altrender: Oversize Legal Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 15.25)
container.label: Computer Disks [31924113901924]

container.label: Computer Disks [31924113901924]

container.label: Computer Disks [31924113901924]

container.label: Computer Disks [31924113901924]

did.unittitle: Dr. Konstantin Frank Vinifera Wine Cellars Video Clips

container.altrender: Oversize Legal Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 15.25)
container.label: Computer Disks [31924113901924]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Six video clips; articles, history, ines, awards, distributors. Produced by Matthew Davidson.
